ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED. DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT SUBMIT ANY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING CRANITE SHEET GASKETS OR ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PACKING ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF MR. AY ARE SUSTAINED. (PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077. (220)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne T. Acuna, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Philip Duncan

RULING:
DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 3 (premises owner/contractor liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 1, 2, 4 and 5 (strict liability, false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). As pled, plaintiff's cause of action for premises owner/contractor liability, as it relates to contractors, does not require a showing that defendant owned, operated, maintained or controlled the premises. While this likely means that there is no substantive difference between plaintiffs' causes of action for negligence and premises owner/contractor liability, that is not a basis to grant summary adjudication and is more appropriately handled by the trial judge through jury instructions or otherwise. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability, false representation and civil battery and his request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel Fox, Esq., of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
Anne T. Acuna, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Philip Duncan

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not submit any admissible evidence creating a triable issue he was exposed to asbestos-containing Cranite sheet gaskets or asbestos-containing packing attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. (Prevailing party is to prepare an order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
